what_it_does: Declares a planned surface specification for view A10, a multi-graph picker under journey J9, mapping it to the canvas marquee address and prescribing build targets and design-token sources.
observations: Generated surface-spec with a provenance note explaining directory and address semantics
View A10 represents the CAN-multigraph capability and targets desktop and mobile
Home journey is J9 with no cross-journey links
build_to ties the spec to the canvas component, a design-lint command, and a token-to-CSS pipeline
Status is planned and no code symbols are present
standouts: Maps the surface to the ui://canvas/marquee address
build_to combines component target, lint gate, and token-derived styling in one surface record
conventions: Directory path follows a journey spine (J9/A10)
journeys and cross_journeys are used as address-carrying references
Addresses are concrete and the file warns against inventing them
build_to separates component, lint, and token concerns
concerns: Status is planned, so the spec is not yet implemented
cross_journeys is empty despite the view name implying multi-graph interactions
The design_lint command is informal and appears truncated ('--fail-on (the FORM gate)')
Represents contains a single capability while the title suggests broader scope
notes: This is the planned surface contract for a desktop/mobile multi-graph picker view tied to the canvas marquee under journey J9.
questions: What exact behavior does the CAN-multigraph capability denote?
Which specific rules does the design_lint FORM gate enforce?
How does ui://canvas/marquee relate to other canvas addresses?
Will cross_journeys remain empty once the feature ships?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a multi-graph picker surface for journey J9, and the file does so by declaring identifiers, addresses, build targets, and token sources, matching its purpose.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a single, authoritative surface record that links a product capability (multi-graph selection) to a concrete UI address and build pipeline, so implementations can be traced back to the intended journey and design system.
novelty: The spec embeds build-to instructions (a lint gate and a token pipeline) directly inside a surface record, treating the surface as both a design contract and an implementation pointer.
contribution: A concrete mapping between the CAN-multigraph capability, the A10 view, the J9 journey, and the canvas marquee address, plus build constraints that anchor implementation to the canvas component and the token-derived design system.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J9/A10.json is a planned surface specification for the A10 multi-graph picker view under journey J9, binding it to the ui://canvas/marquee address and prescribing canvas component build, design lint, and token-based styling.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to record the design and build contract for a cross-platform graph picker surface so that implementation can be traced to journey J9 and the canvas design system.
